title: John C. Plant officer biography (Howmet Aerospace IR)
used_for: CEO & Executive Chairman John Plant (age 72): director since 2016, Exec Chairman since 2017, CEO 2019-20, Co-CEO 2020-21, CEO 2021-present; ex-TRW Automotive. Ken Giacobbe EVP & CFO. Context: 2020 Arconic separation created Howmet

title: RTX to take ~$3 billion charge on Pratt & Whitney GTF powder-metal problem (CNBC / RTX)
used_for: GTF powder-metal contamination (PW1100 parts made Q4'15-Q3'21); RTX ~$3B Q3'23 pre-tax charge (net 51% share), program cost up to ~$7B; ~600-700 incremental shop visits 2023-26; avg ~350 A320neo aircraft grounded/yr through 2026, peak Q1 2024 -> structural SPARES tailwind for hot-section component makers

title: GE Aerospace announces first quarter 2024 results
used_for: Q1'24 orders $11.0B (+34%), revenue $8.1B (+15%); commercial services +39%, internal shop-visit revenue +35%, spare-parts sales +25% on improved material availability; defense orders $3.0B (+34%) -> engine aftermarket running hot (the demand pull behind Howmet hot-section content)

title: Safran publishes full-year 2023 results
used_for: Civil aftermarket +32.9% (CFM56 spares + LEAP RPFH); recurring operating income EUR3,166M (+31.5%); FY2024 guide ~EUR27.4B rev -> second large-engine franchise confirming sustained aftermarket strength

title: RTX reports Q1 2024 results
used_for: Pratt & Whitney sales +23% (commercial OE +64%, commercial aftermarket +9%) -> GTF aftermarket/spares ramp still building into 2024

title: IATA full-year 2023 air passenger traffic (RPK) results
used_for: FY2023 total RPK = 94.1% of 2019; Q4'23 = 98.2%; Dec-2023 = 97.5%; domestic FY2023 = 103.9% of 2019 -> air travel fully recovered, supporting engine flying-hours and shop visits

title: Airbus reports first quarter 2024 results
used_for: Q1'24 deliveries 142 (116 A320-family); backlog 8,626; 'making progress towards rate 75/mo [A320 family] in 2026' -> narrow-body ramp still pointed up (rising LEAP/PW1100 ship-set demand)

title: Boeing reports first quarter 2024 results
used_for: Q1'24 deliveries 83 (67x737, 13x787, 3x767) - lowest since mid-2021; ran below the FAA 38/mo cap (post Jan-5-2024 Alaska MAX-9 door-plug); Q1 net loss $355M -> near-term new-build narrow-body volume headwind (the principal cyclical risk into 2024)

title: Airbus and Boeing report December and full-year 2023 orders and deliveries (Forecast International / Flight Plan)
used_for: Combined Airbus+Boeing YE2023 backlog ~14,814 aircraft (Airbus 8,598 = 11.7 yrs; Boeing 6,216 = 11.8 yrs); 2023 deliveries Airbus 735 / Boeing 528 -> ~decade-plus locked demand for airfoils/fasteners/structures

title: Lockheed Martin details 2024 F-35 delivery plans (Defense Security Monitor)
used_for: F-35 2024 delivery plan 75-110 jets, build rate held ~156/yr (TR-3 storage = timing not demand) -> steady military engine/airfoil demand; corroborates HWM defense +12% / F-35 +27%

title: Class 8 truck market outlook for 2024 (FleetOwner; cites FTR March-2024 forecast)
used_for: FTR March-2024 forecast ~248,800 Class 8 units for 2024, down ~26% vs 2023 (~332,000); freight overcapacity, recession-like macro -> 2024 down-cycle signal for Howmet Forged Wheels (partial offset to aerospace strength)

title: Russian titanium supply / VSMPO-AVISMA dependence (The Air Current / AeroTime background)
used_for: Pre-war Boeing/Airbus sourced ~80%/~60% titanium from Russia's VSMPO; deliberately excluded from Western sanctions to protect aerospace; Airbus diversified to ~20% Russian by 2023 -> titanium/casting/labor as a system ramp constraint (caps volume but supports pricing/share for qualified suppliers)
